headline: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of AI concentration risks
summary: In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella warned of the growing concentration in the handful of artificial intelligence companies leading the AI boom.  Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Semafor business and finance editor Liz Hoffman break down where Nadella's biggest concerns lie. Private developers Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) are set for mega-IPOs in late 2026, with Microsoft owning 27% of the latter's for-profit arm.

content.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of AI concentration risks
content.description:
In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella warned of the growing concentration in the handful of artificial intelligence companies leading the AI boom.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Semafor business and finance editor Liz Hoffman break down where Nadella's biggest concerns lie.

Private developers Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) are set for mega-IPOs in late 2026, with Microsoft owning 27% of the latter's for-profit arm.
content.summary: In an interview with the Wall Street Journal, Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella warned of the growing concentration in the handful of artificial intelligence companies leading the AI boom.  Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman and Semafor business and finance editor Liz Hoffman break down where Nadella's biggest concerns lie. Private developers Anthropic (ANTH.PVT) and OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) are set for mega-IPOs in late 2026, with Microsoft owning 27% of the latter's for-profit arm.

headline: Chevron (CVX) Signs 20 Year Microsoft Deal To Power Texas AI Data Center
summary: Chevron (NYSE:CVX) has entered a 20 year agreement to supply natural gas power to a major Microsoft AI data center in West Texas. The project, known as Project Kilby, involves Chevron, Microsoft and energy infrastructure firm Joulent. The deal uses Chevron's resource position in the Permian Basin to provide electricity tailored to AI data center power needs. For readers tracking Chevron, this move extends the company beyond its traditional upstream and downstream oil and gas focus into...

summary: TORONTO, June 22, 2026--Vena, the Microsoft-native platform for Orchestrated Planning, Decisioning and Close, today announced it has been recognized with 19 top ranks and 21 leading positions across four peer groups in The Planning Survey 26 by BARC. headline: ICON selects Microsoft as a preferred technology partner to power AI-enabled clinical development
